Mr. William Barnwell is truly exceptional and highly knowledgeable in the law. I was wrongfully accused of a criminal charge and urgently needed someone to represent me. After reading numerous positive reviews online, I chose to hire him—and I’m so grateful I did.

From our very first meeting, he understood both me and the details of my case with clarity. He explained the legal process thoroughly and guided me every step of the way. At one point, I was so overwhelmed and stressed—especially as a heart patient—that I considered pleading guilty just to get it over with, even though I was innocent.

Mr. Barnwell, however, remained calm and confident. He respectfully disagreed with my impulse to give up and instead pursued a strategic approach to prove my innocence. Thanks to his dedication and expertise, my case was dismissed and justice was served.

To me, Mr. Barnwell is nothing short of a miracle. I don't have enough words to express my gratitude. Please—if you’re in need of a strong, honest, and capable attorney—trust him. He will fight for you. May God bless him and his family.

Mr. Russi lacks competence and reading comprehension skills. He was sub-contracted as a business associate to help a family member's independent healthcare worker ensure proper HIPAA protocols, except he doesn't specialize in health law and basically the healthcare business at which he was employed/subcontracted as a business associate totally botched our very simple and routine HIPAA request. We had no choice but to report the business to the Federal Office for Civil Rights (OCR) for multiple and repeated HIPAA violations, and they have been under investigation for a year. The nature/legality of Mr. Russi's relationship with this business is also being investigated by the OCR. Additionally, as a result of Mr. Russi's lack of basic legal knowledge, we were forced to sue the business and they settled for $40,000. This man is clueless. UPDATE November 2022: The Federal Office for Civil Rights (OCR) completed their HIPAA investigation and found that Mr. Russi retaliated against us (which is unlawful under HIPAA regulations) and also falsely held himself out as a "business associate" with the independent healthcare office when in fact he did not have the requisite contractual arrangements with the office to claim that he was a "business associate".

First I would like to say I didn't want to give a 1 star rating but I couldn't write my review unless I picked a star and I felt it was important to get this out to hopefully help others.
John Russi from the firm Moore Penna & Associates  is a disgrace to be practicing law.
When we started with him the first thing we asked Mr. Russi can we win this case he said Yes.  We than asked how much will this cost he said approximately $5,000-$10,000 the most unless it goes to trial than it could cost $30,000.
We told him we don't have that kind of money he preceded to tell us it won't get that fair or last that long  not with our type of case and the evidence we had.
After a year and a half and $32,000 later with no trial we Lost the case and our savings.   Please understand we aren't bitter because we Lost... it is how we Lost.
He told us the only way we could of lost  is...."Either the judge is stupid and we know that isn't so or someone called in a favor the fix was in".
Yep you heard correctly someone asked for a favor and we didn't get a fair ruling from the Judge.  Call us naive but we were in shock that this truly goes on in the court system.
So we asked what he was going to due to make it right he kept saying it will cost thousands of dollars to go forward and I know you can't afford to go forward. You would think he would want to go forward to make it right.
Later we find out our friends called Mr Russi and told him to due the right thing and go back to court.
They asked him how much will it cost he again said thousands they said 1 or 2 thousands he said I just want to be done with this case I'm tired they couldn't believe what he said.
I know this sounds like a fictional story but I have every email that Russi had sent us plus in his own words how terrible this was that the Fix was in and we didn't get a fair ruling.
Russi said the opinion of the Judge was terrible she couldn't of written it her self knowing this he walks away feeling no obligation to make it right.
He doesn't care about his clients or the oath he took when he became an attorney.   Believe this as of today he is still asking for money he feels he put in a lot more hours then we paid him.
Mr John Russi didn't fulfill his Fiduciary Duty or upheld the Constitution that states everyone gets a fair and honest ruling instead he walks away knowing our case was Fixed his words.
To the person that gave John Russi a 5 star review without putting their name on it (which is odd) never stating they were a client.    My review is on our experience with Mr. Russi as our attorney and how he couldn't  stand up against corruption in the courts because he was concerned what that would do to his career in the future....his words.
I had never written a review before but I'm so passionate to stop this man from ever hurting and taking advantage of Senior Citizens or anyone else and not due what he was paid to do.
To think the Court  is not built on the truth makes me sick and to know others can influence the outcome of a ruling by just asking for a favor is truly Pathetic and against our constitutional rights.
Everyone should be accountable especially the attorney that chose to turn his  head.
We will continue to put our story out there so maybe one day it can make a difference.

When I met Avis over a year ago I was broken soul when it came to the process of Character and Fitness. Having gone to both the district committee and the standing committee with another attorney and not having been approved I thought about giving up. Then one of my most dearest of friends told me to talk to Avis. Funny she had told me to talk to him 2 years earlier, I wish I had listened.

Avis sat me down, didn’t sugar coat a thing and told me that what I had done prior to him was not enough to get me through this process. He took me under his wing and in the year we waited for the BLE, worked with me to get things properly in order for me to even have a shot at getting licensed. Between texts, emails and phone calls I must have annoyed this man more then any other human, but he never turned me away or made me feel like I was.

The day of my BLE hearing I asked him what my chances were, his response “had you come to me first I would be more confident but I promise you, you are as prepared as anyone can be now” I had my hearing and when it was all over we were told we had to wait for a response. As I was walking out of his office he gave me a hug told me I did great and we parted ways. The next time I heard from him was when he told me I was approved for my license.

This man is literally a god send, I would recommend him to anyone who is going to have to go through the character and fitness process. No matter how small or how big make Avis your first call, I promise you he will prepare you and work tirelessly to make sure you have a the tools to get through this process. He is the best character and fitness attorney in the State of Michigan. Thank you Avis and Merry Christmas to you and your family. Much love.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Sorry to hear you feel our work is unclear and wihthout supporting documents. We always strive to make every client happy with transactional work and contracts like yours, and we do not rest until that is satisfied; even if it requires substantial editing. Without explaining with any particularity or explaining the issue in anything other than conclusory terms, you asked for a refund of work I did over a month ago. I then came to find out that your circumstances changed to cause you not to need my work any longer. The issue is you already consumed the service and it is not my fault your plans changed.
Your review is comical. I would not do work for you again, and I’d caution any attorney from taking you on as a client for reason of your insincerity and lack of character. It's simply pathetic that you'd attempt to disparage us to get your money back when, in fact, your circumsances changed and you have buyers regret.

reviews.text: I found The Schipper Law Group simply through a Google search, but how lucky I did! Hooray for The Schipper Law Group! If you need this type of help, can't recommend them highly enough!

reviews.ownerResponse: Unfortunatley, we cannot respond to all phone inquiries because we are very busy providing legal representation to clients. When asked why he was refusing to visit me in jail after MONTHS of ignoring me and refusing to give me my discovery packet, he replied "your son is a drug dealer. What do you expect? Him not to get in trouble"?  (This was my paid lawyer!) He would then tell my family he would visit me with my discovery before sentencing or trial. Yet, he would NEVER show up to me in jail for 5 months! He never gave me discovery, he kept all $7000, and he even refused to read the circuit court files that reminded him that we got the most serious charges thrown out months ago in district court. That was his job.  If I hadn't spoke up to judge Sharonowski, I would have gotten 2 years in prison using Ernie Robinette Jr. Instead of probation and time served. He actually came out in the hallway to tell me I was going to prison on charges that we had gotten dropped months ago in district due to drug lab results. I simply reminded the judge myself and everything changed to probation. I should have sued this guy for my money back. He will physically show up. But that is it. Stay Away! (I wish I had the guts to take action when I was younger)

reviews.text:
We met Brian after our serious auto accident July 2021, where my husband and I were BOTH injured!! Ambulances took us both to the hospital, where I was rushed into trauma surgery for serious leg and ankle injuries.

In seconds our peaceful lives became chaotic! We were getting the trauma care we needed – but there were so many fears and questions… NOW WHAT? What did our insurance cover? What we our legal rights? How are we going to recover from this situation?

We needed someone to trust. Someone highly experienced in personal injury law - who could advise and help us both.

Brian Bourbeau came highly recommended as a trusted, ethical, and highly respected personal injury attorney. He met with us soon after, and we never doubted that he had our best interests in mind. After working with Brian and his firm for over a year, we absolutely felt like his #1 priority!

Brian also made things easy for us to understand and clearly explained what our rights were under current Michigan law. He was honest and truthful with us, and answered our questions as quickly as possible.

Brian is also easy to talk with. Even during the most stressful times in our recovery, I could call, text, and or email him and he’d respond to me quickly and personally! (We never had to jump through hoops.) Of course, he also has excellent staff who are friendly and professional.

Overall, his communication skills are exceptional – for any industry – let alone the legal field. We felt he cared about us personally, as well as our legal cases. And Brian continues to protect our physical, mental, and financial well-being.

He fought for what we need NOW and, in the FUTURE, so we have as much independence as possible. Both our cases were settled within a year of our MVA! We’re finally moving forward.

If you, or someone you love is injured in an auto accident, don’t hesitate to call Brian Bourbeau.
reviews.ownerResponse: Emily - Thank you for taking the time to write such a heartfelt and thoughtful review.  It is much appreciated.  I am glad that we were able to help. Brian

Peters was the ONLY attorney that didn't ask specifics about my case but rather explained how the disability process worked. After having a broken C2, broken arm and closed head injury my hearing lasted 2 and a half hours (normal hearings are a half hour). Despite having all documents to the judge well before the hearing, my judge was very thorough and it was an exhausting process. Mr. Peters remained the ultimate professional and a determination was made immediately following the hearing in my favor. I don't believe I would have won the case despite the medical documents providing the necessary information. I would highly recommend Mr. Peters to anyone seeking an understanding of the disability process  no matter how easy your case may seem.

reviews.text: My first impression was amazing however after being blown off and sent to voicemails for over a week, I highly recommend not using this agency. They were courteous, efficient, and demonstrated excellent communication and determination. I would gladly recommend them to my friends and family. Thank you, team!

Zero-Star Review for Robert Huth at Kirk, Huth, Lange & Badalamenti, PLC**

I feel compelled to express my profound dissatisfaction with Robert Huth's performance as the city attorney for the Charter Township of Shelby. In 2023, I initiated a formal complaint and was assured of a supportive meeting with Mr. Huth by the township supervisor, promised during a recorded call. Unfortunately, this meeting never transpired, and my attempts to communicate with the township supervisor were met with prolonged radio silence. This lack of engagement persisted into the next year, 2024, raising serious concerns about the transparency and integrity of the township’s leadership. Based on my experiences, I suspect there may be deeper issues of misconduct or corruption that are being concealed. I believe an investigation by an outside agency is necessary to uncover the full extent of what might be serious corruption within our township. This is purely my opinion, formed from the distressing lack of accountability and responsiveness I have encountered.

It was not until 2024 that I received a callback from Mr. Huth. The interaction was unexpectedly brief and unsettlingly nonchalant, as if prior discussions had taken place or resolutions offered neither of which was the case. This approach not only hindered progress on pressing issues but also left me feeling overlooked and undervalued as a concerned resident.

From my perspective, the level of engagement and urgency demonstrated by Mr. Huth falls significantly short of what is expected from someone in such a pivotal role. The responsiveness and dedication to resolving township matters efficiently and transparently are crucial, yet seem to be lacking. My experiences have led me to question whether our community's needs are genuinely being prioritized.

Based on these interactions, I believe that our township would greatly benefit from a change in this position. Someone with a robust commitment to community issues and a proactive approach to communication is sorely needed. We deserve a city attorney who not only addresses our concerns promptly but does so with the seriousness and attention they warrant.

In conclusion, I am of the opinion that Mr. Huth is not the right fit for his current role as city attorney. It is my sincere hope that our township will consider making a change that better aligns with the needs and expectations of its 80,000 residents. We need leadership that is both responsive and responsible qualities that are essential for the health and well-being of our community.

Mike Taylor was fantastic. He helped me through my estate when though I live in another state. He did a great job representing and advising me asking the way. He and his associates treated me with kindness and access compassion. I highly recommend Mike and his firm.

Elyse distinguishes herself with a rare convergence of intellect, dedication, and genuine compassion. She doesn’t merely practice law — she brings it to life through thoughtful, unwavering advocacy. There is a particular calm that settles in when you realize you are, unequivocally, in capable hands. The legal world can feel vast and unnerving, a maze of uncertainty and endless paperwork. Yet Elyse brought a rare clarity to the chaos. She approached my case not as a file to be processed, but as a human story to be understood. She asked the questions that mattered, listened with uncommon attentiveness, and carried my concerns with a genuine sense of empathy. Her confidence was never performative; it was steady, rooted in a deep well of knowledge and experience that inspired trust from the outset. Through every twist and turn, she remained present—explaining, strategizing, and, most of all, caring. The outcome she achieved was more than a legal victory; it was a personal one, reflecting the integrity and loyalty she brings to her work. Attorneys like Elyse quietly redefine what true advocacy looks like. For her guidance, I am profoundly grateful—and I recommend her without hesitation. A million hugs and thank yous! ☺️

I come from a family of very accomplished attorneys. My family referred me to an attorney who then referred me to Mr. Garmo. The referring attorney stated to me that my case was one that is very time consuming and difficult particularly in the notoriously frustrating Oakland County MI Judicial System but if I wanted to pursue it Brian Garmo was his highest recommendation.

* I chose to use a secondary google account to remain somewhat anonymous as I am a business professional not wanting to broadcast a very traumatic experience but I am a 37 year old male*

Case(s) Synopsis:

I came to Mr. Garmo heartbroken and manipulated. My girlfriend of 3 years verbally assaulted me for about 2 years whittling away my self worth  and confidence while demanding (and my embarrassingly obliging) money to temporarily alleviate her assault on me. I still pray and worry for her as I loved her. Watching someone you love attempt suicide and end up in mental institutions is devastating to bear. My ex-gf choose to end our relationship by moving out one snowy morning while I took my son to school which is usually about a 2 hour round trip but was roughly 3.5 hrs that day. She broke many things, stole the items she wanted, put framed pictures of her up, stole my and my sons personal information from my filing cabinet, and in her distain for Christianity put devil masks and other weird trinkets in my Christmas storage tote. The only thing I could not handle was she stole the 2 Mini Golden Doodles I bought my 9 year old son for Christmas the month before. She blocked and ignored me and I went to her work to retrieve my puppies who were locked in her car during an 8 hour shift at a nursing home during the winter. I was told to leave because unbeknownst to me she had stolen the registration and registered them in her name as well as filed a PPO that was some how granted Ex Parte with pictures from injuries she claimed came from me. I am a kind gentle person and have never lifted a hand in violence against a female in my life but she weaved a good tale and the judge fed into at the time. I knew the only way to get my puppies back and also clear my name was formally through the court system.

Case Verdict(s):
1.PPO:

When my family first contacted Brian it was about three weeks from my court date and we were extremely worried about our situation. I was facing a misdemeanor charge for my fake ID after it was mistakenly given to a police officer when I was in a car accident. Along with the misdemeanor, I also had two traffic tickets, that could’ve resulted in me having to restart my provisional license. Brian was very honest about potential outcomes, and discussed worst case scenario situations. It’s also important to note how knowledgeable he is about the law, and the court process, as he was extremely educated and answered all of our questions immediately over the phone. He also is fantastic at answering his emails, unlike other lawyers Brian responded to questions even after typical office hours within minutes.
At my court date, Brian showed up very well prepared, and already knew the outcome of the case before it had begun in the courtroom. He was able to make a deal with the prosecutor and completely dismiss my misdemeanor charge, along with one of my traffic violations, and reduce the other violation to a civil infraction and a very small fee. In the end, I only paid $135 for violations I easily could’ve paid $2k+ for without Brian.
Every penny you spend to pay for Brian is one less penny you’ll have to spend in legal fees and potential record damaging charges. I can not speak highly enough of Brian and my experience with him!

I contacted Brian for a legal matter.  He was forthcoming with information, made me feel at ease, and was swift in taking care of the issue. We sincerely apologize for the lack of follow-up and communication you experienced after reaching out to Mr. Smith and our firm. It's clear we need to review and improve our procedures for handling inquiries and ensuring every potential client receives a timely and clear response.

We would appreciate the chance to make amends and offer you the support you were initially seeking. If you are still in need of legal advice or services, please contact us directly, and we will ensure your case is given the attention it deserves right from the start.

Sincerely,
The Law Firm of Michael J. Smith and Associates, PLLC

reviews.text: I chose Muthu because he is the youngest forensic lawyer scientist in the US and the second in Michigan. He knew exactly how to defeat the blood alcohol and drug issues in Court. The evidence was not admitted during the trial and the charges were ultimately dismissed! I am very pleased with having Muthu represent me and I would highly recommend him 👍

My attorney Muthu Veerappan assisted with our business formation and assisted us through a business dispute. We successfully resolved the matter in a short amount of time and were very pleased with the results. He’s a fantastic attorney with excellent communication skills, customer service, and great negotiation skills.

I have know Muthu for years, since he was an insurance defense attorney at Novara Tesijia McDonald & Baas PLLC. He is well versed in auto claims, bodily injury, and negligence claims. He has helped me many times in the past and he is always transparent, communicative and clear with goals. You should definitely give Muthu a call for a free consultation.
Highly recommend. Everything went to the positive outcome. Would definitely use again if any future problems requiring legal representation. Highly trusted many thanks.

This is my honest opinion per my actual case this year.
Muthu Veerappan had my case and I was looking for civil lawsuit with contingency base fee, but he strongly recommended to start with PPO first. From at some point, I couldn’t stop thinking he did it because he wanted the upfront money for PPO as it doesn’t matter whether it is successful or not, differ from contingency based. I have many reasons why I believe that. For example, because my main purpose was for civil lawsuit, I asked several times when he would start to prepare for it but he continued to avoid answering the questions. (We communicated mostly by email). Before I signed  the contract, I asked and he said I have a very strong case to win, but as I worried per his reactions, he did not even start any single thing for the civil suit and he just informed me that he terminated the contract without any discussion with me as soon as PPO was denied.(I will say more at the bottom, so read below). Another example is that I explained what happened but he just listened what I said and I do not remember he asked any single question to find out more or understand the case better. I thought he would do later as we work together but never happened.

Before I signed the contracts, I asked timelines of the PPO filing and civil suit, he said he would file PPO in 2 weeks but after signed everything was changed. It took about a week in general to get his respond on my email and the PPO was ended to file about 2 months later. The judge was like… “why now?” because PPO filed about a few months later after the incident. Muthu Veerappan took all those time and provided major reason to be declined in my opinion, and I felt I wasted time and money but most importantly lost a “potential” chance of accepting PPO by delaying too long.

Another thing I want you to share is about contracts. When he sent me the 2 contracts for PPO and civil suit, I had to point out several things that are not correct or didn’t make sense per what he told me and what we talked. I didn’t think he even reviewed before sent. Anyway he didn’t clear out some of things still but I just let it go because I had email about that.
One thing you really have to pay attention to the contract is (at least in my contract) there was a statement saying that he(the lawyer) can terminate the contract at anytime without any reason. I asked him for explanation and he said it is just when for some customers don’t pay fees/owed or communicate, and it wouldn’t apply to my case as I paid all already and seem to actively communicate. However, as I mentioned above, as soon as PPO was declined, he terminated the contracts without any discussion with me but just informed me and refused to do any further. Again, he did NOTHING for civil suit that was why I was looking for a lawyer. This was really shocking to me and hurt me so badly but I just couldn’t do anything.

As I share above, I had so many bad impressions and experiences with Muthu Veerappan, and he didn’t look professional to me at all. I was desperately looking for help on the incident but I felt like he just took an advantage from me  instead of helping me as a lawyer. I am so regretting and I would’ve never signed the contract if I knew it.

Had consultation with her about possibly switching attorneys in a messy divorce and she was frustrated with me and insensitive of the situation. She said she did not want to deal with my case.

Update in response to her justification of her actions:
She was not interested in listening to what actually happened, she instead assumed that it was the typical drama in a divorce with custody, equally blaming both parents but she had no idea of what measures I had already took nor did she ask. She spoke as if my situation was beneath her. I had higher expectations of you from your reviews. Too many times in that short conversation you told me things were ridiculous and what I can’t do or can’t prove without asking me anything. When getting off the phone with you I felt more helpless then when I called you originally. You have no idea of the actions i or my ex have taken and for you to assume that I took or take part in it is ridiculous. My situation is about a parent going out of their way to make my relationship with my children a living hell. Years of being in a violent situation and finally standing up for myself-wanting him and his family to not try to ruin my relationship with my children and miss Josephine considers it her business to tell me I’m part of the problem because  that’s just her hunch. Our world was turned upside down due to a denied homeowners insurance claim. We had no idea where to go or what to do. After speaking with Mrs Annie Marie Earls one time, she was the first person to give us hope and direction. To make all this even harder it was at the peak of Covid-19’s shut down of the whole state. Mrs Earls was very attentive in understanding our situation and compassionate about our loss, not having a legal background ourselves. Mrs Earls was able to explain the process in words we could understand. She was very open and transparent in all steps and extremely committed to our best interest. Due to Mrs Earls tireless work, ease to contact and willing to take the time to ensure we were aware and comfortable in the process. We highly recommend her services. Mrs Annie Marie Earls made us feel better throughout this difficult situation and supported us every step of the way. Hopefully we are never in this situation again, but if we were Mrs Earls is the first person we would call. We can not thank her enough.

We had a major fire loss December 23, 2019. Our insurance was initially helpful in covering funds for immediate housing and a small amount of cash for personal needs. Things went down hill quickly and by mid January we knew we were in an adversarial situation. We began to look for an appropriate law firm experienced in situations like ours. Our first contact with Melamed, Levitt, Milanowski & Earls was a lengthy phone conversation with Annie Earls. She listened, asked pertinent questions, helped define the battle we were in, and provided assurance our fight was winnable. We interviewed two law firms and are very pleased we choose Ms. Earls.
We were not able to start rebuilding until August 2020. We moved back in in February of this year. Our fight included Examinations Under Oath and multiple court filings. Annie helped lead us the whole way. The experience was long, frustrating, and difficult. The financial and other personal information the insurance company required seemed endless. We received our final payment from the insurance company the middle of November 2021. Annie was with us the whole way and was instrumental in our ability to reach an adequate settlement.

Attorney Annie & Her Firm were very much experienced and professional handling our case. She was there for us every time we tried to reach her and her services were very much satisfied. We wish the firm the best of luck in the future and we will always recommend MLMF to our friends and family!

reviews.text:
Finding the right attorney when you’re facing divorce is hard. Finding one when you’re permanently disabled, caring for an autistic child, and trying to safely separate from a covert narcissist feels nearly impossible. But from the very first phone call—on a Saturday morning—I knew I had found someone different in Jodi Switalski of Switalski Law.

She didn’t just bring her extensive experience as both a judge and an attorney—she brought heart. Her background in psychology and addictions meant she didn’t just understand my case—she understood me. As someone with over 20 years of sobriety, finding an attorney who truly “got it” was vital. Jodi did. She still does.

She never once made me feel like I was too much or that my case was too messy. From the beginning, she saw my strength, reminded me of it when I forgot, and helped me fight for what mattered most: the wellbeing of my autistic child and the ability to stay grounded in integrity, even through high-conflict chaos.

During brutal mediations and court battles, Jodi and Claire (then a brilliant law clerk, now a lawyer) worked tirelessly, holding steady through the storms created by covert narcissism. They never flinched. They strategized, they supported, and they stood by me when I wanted to give up. And Judy, Jodi’s legal assistant, was just as amazing—responsive, kind, and always on top of things.

Jodi is honest and direct about what you’re up against, but she doesn’t let it deter her. She stands firm in the courtroom and never loses her moral compass. She never allowed me to compromise my values, especially when I felt tempted to just survive and surrender. She reminded me that I was becoming stronger for my son, and that I had a voice—and someone in my corner.

Even though my divorce is finalized, I still cry today because I know she still gets me. She didn’t just check a box and close a file—because that’s not who she is. With Jodi, you’re not just a client. You’re someone she cares about. Truly.

I’ll continue to hire her for what lies ahead, because while I can do it alone, I’m so much better with her. Jodi is not just a lawyer—she’s a warrior, a protector, and truly, an angel on earth.

If you’re wondering whether she’s the right fit—listen to your gut. She changed my life. I know she can change yours, too.
reviews.ownerResponse: Karin - The strength and courage you showed in the face of unimaginable difficulty is truly inspiring. Together, we walked through one of the most painful and pivotal times in your life. We are honored to be the ones that fought for your safety, peace and the future of you and your child. Thank you for telling your story so openly. When survivors speak out, it helps them reclaim their voice while creating awareness for others who are walking similar paths. You have a powerful voice Karin. Thank you for speaking your truth.

reviews.text: Unfortunately, this was the worst legal experience I have ever had. I have worked with the best firms and decided to try a smaller firm. I retained Morgan for my family law matter. She lacked the confidence, maturity, and aggressiveness that I wanted. I explicitly explained that my ex would not come prepared & this would be an easy case. I could have done this on my own but Hired counsel so I didn’t have to deal with him. He didn’t come prepared. It doesn’t get much easier than that. Morgan was passive aggressive-she told me about “issues” only after I had voiced mine first regarding her representation after reflecting on it. She initially told me she thought the hearing went “great” and after I voiced concerns she told me “my talking and interrupting hurt our argument” when I already won 2/3. That’s passive aggressive. Isn’t that something I should be told, as a paying client, after the hearing when we spoke and not coming on the heels of my email expressing discontent?  She said a whole five words during an hour zoom meeting and I kept emailing her during the zoom telling her to bring this up. She even put the blame on me for getting the days mixed up of our hearing when I sent the zoom Info and she didn’t read it. The email heading even said “see you in a few”. It’s not the clients job to keep track of an attorneys agenda. Read documents that your clients send you to double check. I’m not paying to be a secretary when I have active representation. I even provided “work product” to Morgan that came from my previously $550/hr counsel to use.  She billed me for emails sent during the zoom call (even ones where the referee was asking me to contact her) which I was already billed for the zoom call and billed for emails that she read but never responded to. I tried to bring this up to the Daniel and no response. If you want excellent representation go with a larger firm with a well known attorney. I only went with this firm because my case was so easy, I didn’t want to do the talking but wanted my voice to be heard through my attorney. I wrote her a long opening since the other side gets to talk and she said “it was helpful” but again after paragraph and paragraph of info, she only said “mom pays for camp and school”?!  That’s it. I wanted my ex to be held accountable for a frivolous motion. You pay for what you get with representation! You can’t buy confidence or experience. A newer attorney lacks the skill.This is more disturbing. She even suggested in the beginning that we should have a “male” counselor meet with my ex because “it didn’t sound like my ex respected women” and she thought having another male attorney would be helpful in gaining respect from my ex. She should demand respect and not need a “prop” to obtain results.RED FLAG That’s the difference.She wanted to have me pay another attorney from the firm that was a male (she recommended Daniel) to have a meeting with me and my ex. Women are still oppressed because of that mentality. We don’t need a man to demand respect towards women. We don’t ask for respect, we demand it. It helps to have an attorney that is friendly with the judge or has a relationship with but not hire a male to demand respect from another man. Take this as review as you want but I’d never recommend them. They don’t call back/follow up when you voice concerns.This was someone else’s concern in the comments. They are responsive up until that point but doesn’t instill trust in client/attorney relationship.I contacted Daniel and he told me if I wanted to discuss the matter than to let him know.I sent several emails & a phone call. Nothing. Don’t waste your time; and coming from someone that has worked with the best civil rights attorney (Deb Gordon), family law (Honigman), and business matters (Jaffe). Pay the extra $200 an hour and get someone that is connected in the field and has confidence in themselves and their capability. It’s a night and day experience. You only get one shot in court and we all want to feel like we are vindicated and your counsel is able and competent to fight for you.

reviews.text:
Brace yourselves, this is going to be a long, glowing review.  I have never been through anything before.  I was injured, (not in a car accident), the law firms that advertise heavily on television rejected my claim.  David’s office did not.  I spoke to David personally a few times, always professional, respectful, and empathic.

My injury was inherently not my fault and I endured costs, change of daily activities for several weeks, and pain.

David matched me with attorney Josh Haller, what a great supporter.  I explained to him how clearly I was uncomfortable with the whole process, but felt I should at least be made whole.  I had to pay copays and deductibles and medical devices that I never would have been subject to, if it weren’t for the negligence of the defendant party.

Josh advocated for me.  His assistant, Dawn, what a pleasure to speak with and would answer my emails within seconds.

I thank you all.  Be sure I am always going to speak your praises.

reviews.text:
I ended up being sued by a former landlord for back rent they claimed was owed for a lease I never resigned. Around tax time 2025, I got a letter saying the state was holding my refund and would be giving it to the debt collectors as payment unless I filed for bankruptcy. My previous roommate had the same issue a few years back & recommended Acclaim Legal Services to file a Chapter 7 bankruptcy so I could get my refund back (they were holding it for 4 weeks).

I reached out to Acclaim & everyone that helped me was so kind and knowledgeable. Giovanna, one of Acclaim’s paralegals, & Kelly, Acclaim’s office clerk, helped me with paperwork and all the questions I had including telling me the entire process of how filing bankruptcy works & never lost patience even through all the emails I sent with tax forms, pay stubs & what not. My attorney, Bill Johnson, was professional & trustworthy. We had a few calls to converse before court & filing and never once did he make me feel lost or confused or judged. The team knew about the time constraints I had with filing & made sure I got all the information needed to file for Chapter 7 before the state completely seized my refund. I am eternally grateful to them for that!

If you’re in a pinch, I am confident that the associates at Acclaim Legal Services can help you with professionalism, dedication & humanity.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you so much for taking the time to share your story — your kind words truly mean a lot to me and to everyone here at Acclaim. I know how stressful and frustrating that situation must have been, and I’m so glad my team and I could help! Giovanna, Kelly, and I are all grateful for your trust and your recommendation — it means the world to us.

If you ever need anything in the future, please don’t hesitate to reach out. Wishing you all the best ahead!

Warning this company will lie to you and steal your money. By far the worst experience I’ve had with any attorney. They told me on the phone it would take 30 days to evict a woman from my home. It’s been over 4 months and they still haven’t even got me a court date yet. They served a 30 day notice to quit that I had already served prior so it was done twice . When I call no answer when I e mail all I get is replies from his secretary telling me. If I think I can do it better than do it myself. Well I have done it myself in less than half the time it takes them  but I paid for a service and I want what I paid for. I didn’t give them 900$ to send out a notice to quit that I had already done. I will be going to the bar association to report them and show the unprofessional nasty Emails they sent me.

If u are in a position  for anything Estate related with a passing of a loved one this is the firm for you, My scenario is a difficult situation  with siblings fighting  me for  things that have to go thru courts and Mathew Bieber and his staff has continued to put in in a positive way for me to be very confident  in the process.

reviews.text: I am disappointed on the way Mr. Bieber updated my estate planning documents.  Due to a health crisis, we decided to review the documents and found errors in both the Wills and the Second Amendment for the Trust. The Second Codicil for the Will states “this is my first codicil” not second which now voids out this document for both my husband and I. For the Trust, he missed the update on “My Family” either though it was updated on the Second Codicil for the Wills. Three attempts were made to have this corrected with no response. Now I have to either update it myself or engage another attorney.  As for my son’s estate, him and his staff were great and I thank them for that. If you have them update your estate documents, please proof them before signing and leaving the office. Once you leave, it will be very difficult to get them to correct the errors.

Well that turned out to not be true and during the final steps of closing out the estate she told me the money would have to be split with other parties and acted as if we never discussed anything about it. They refuse to accept accountability and delete every comment I leave on their facebook page and refuse to return my calls. They actually sent me an invoice for the multiple calls I made trying to get in touch with them after intentionally avoiding me. If your looking for help to handle someone you loves estate I would not use them. They lie, cheat,  and try to manipulate the law and when they get caught up they'll blame you for everything and avoid you. I regret every going to them.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jennifer, while we do everything we can for our clients, we must adhere to the law and certain ethical standards that were explained to you.  I will also not tolerate anyone speaking to my staff disrespectfully or with certain language - life is too short to get personally upset over money.  Since you have signed your consent to how the estate was ultimately handled, I hope you and your family can move on and find happiness.

Dad’s got a scheduled surgery Thursday. On my phone I have all the papers from The Nakisher Law Firm that lets me walk into the hospital and control the narrative, getting health updates and making health decisions in real time. Family health challenges are stressful enough; don’t be stuck in the waiting room without a voice. Get your parents together with John at the Nakisher Law Firm to get papers while you still have the luxury of time.

Marc is an exception to any rule out there. He is as compassionate as they come and is a brilliant attorney. And when life hits fast, he is waiting to do whatever is needed, at whatever time you need it, to make sure you're protected with the right advice, calmed nerves, and signed documents. And creating a trust is not expensive, contrary to common belief - it should be a mandatory thing for every adult. Keep your assets within your family and out of probate. Do not wait... call them and get your trust filed. You will be able to sleep better knowing you have fulfilled the responsibility of taking care of your family where they need it most.

I absolutely recommend this law firm.

I paid $250 for a consultation in which my legitimate claims of discrimination and retaliation against my employer of 20 years were questioned and belittled as if I were on trial at the law office. I was then advised to settle with my employer for an amount of less than one year of my annual salary because, according to the attorney, my employer is making the case to fire me. Well, of course, that’s why I need a lawyer to counter my employer’s illegal discriminatory and retaliatory conduct. Just because the employer is making a case doesn’t make it legitimate and incontestable—the employer has fabricated charges and retaliated against me for my civil rights complaints.

It is very hard to find passionate and ethical legal representation in employment discrimination cases in Michigan. Unfortunately, this experience added to my psychological distress and I paid $250 for it. I was under a severely mistaken impression that this firm is passionate about employee rights. I was very wrong to assume that. In fact they are apparently buddies with my employer’s general counsel. Lawyer berated and tried to shame me about my salary, as if I haven't earned it through my advanced studies (multiple higher education degrees).

I would not recommend this firm to anyone I remotely care about who is seeking justice against an abusive and unethical employer. Very traumatic experience which added to the trauma of employer’s abuse.

(Note: I'm an ivy-league trained lawyer myself. So I know what good law practice SHOULD look like. This 'aint it.)

reviews.text:
Attorney Erin Rich only looked for shortcuts. I waited months for my hearing which is expected but I actually never spoke with her until the evening before the hearing for maybe 15 minutes. For the 15 minutes we spoke it seemed as if she knew nothing about my case. During the hearing the Vocational Expert stated that I could perform 3 jobs that in reality there was absolutely no way I can perform those, but Ms. Rich asked this "expert" little to no questions. If she did, maybe I would have received a different decision. I received a denial and she represented me for the appeal. I have no idea what she did during the appeal process, but I'm pretty sure it was nothing other than file the paperwork stating that I wanted to appeal which I could have done that myself. I got a second denial and my only other option was to appeal at the Federal level which she was either unwilling or incapable of doing. I was never told which it was. Any time I wanted to speak to Ms. Rich during this entire time I was connected with the Case Manager. My Case Manager was switched 2 or 3 times without even being informed about it. I was never able to actually speak to Ms. Rich. Unfortunately 2 of the Case Manager's that I had came off incompetent and it was extremely frustrating.

The reason why I went with a large company like this was because I thought they would be faster and easier to work with over a small company that has a limited amount of staff. Now that I'm working with an attorney here in my hometown, I wish I would have went that route first. I also think it's crazy that Ms. Rich has promoted to a supervising attorney. My best advice is to go somewhere local because these large companies do not care about you at all.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are truly sorry to hear that you felt frustrated with your experience. Our firm is committed to thoroughly preparing every case, advocating strongly at hearings, and maintaining consistent communication with our clients throughout the process.

Because of confidentiality obligations, we cannot discuss the specifics of any case in a public forum. However, we take concerns like this very seriously. Hearings and appeals—especially at the federal level—can be complex and often involve strategic decisions based on legal standards, medical evidence, and procedural requirements. That said, every client deserves to feel informed, prepared, and supported.

A member of our management team will be reaching out to you directly to better understand your concerns and review your experience.

We appreciate all feedback, as it helps us continue improving our service to the individuals and families who trust us with their cases.

I will probably die before I get any of my money. But Disability Law Group is getting paid no matter if I do or not (that's one document they DIDNT overlook or slack on) - so they feel no urgency. reviews.text:
While this firm was ultimately unable to take on my father's VA case due to its procedural complexity, I cannot say enough good things about how they treated us throughout the process.
Kristina was incredibly responsive, kind, courteous, and patient in all of our email communications. She took the time to review the extensive documentation, communicate clearly about what they were seeing, and - most importantly - she explained why the case presented challenges rather than just sending a form rejection.
Her willingness to provide that extra clarity helped me understand the bigger picture of what we're dealing with and gave me direction for next steps. That kind of professional courtesy and genuine helpfulness is rare, especially when she knew they wouldn't be taking the case.
For veterans with less complicated procedural issues, I would absolutely recommend them. Kristina and this firm clearly care about veterans and treat people with respect and dignity even when they can't help.
Thank you for your time and honesty. It meant more than you know.

Today, September 11, 2025, my son was a  client of AJ. Daoud . My son had  a Zoom call scheduled for 8:30 AM this morning. We had tried reaching out to AJ several times, but there was no response from him or his office. My son went to his office and spoke with his secretary, she was unsuccessful with contacting AJ. as well supposedly. My son reached out to AJ by text and phone to see if he had reviewed the discovery and to discussed the case with him before court date but there was no response.

If you choose not to represent a client, it’s important to be honest and direct with them. You should tell them verbally that you have chosen not to represent them. You shouldn’t have a conference with them and then go over the issues that led to the case, and then stop communicating with them. This is poor representation on your part as an attorney, AJ. There was no reason to be disloyal to a client and give them false pretenses and hopes. This is bad for business as an attorney to leave your client defenseless in the time of need shows poor character as a man.

The best law firm for any case you have, my life was in shambles until I connected with Farris Haddad/ his amazing team. From day one I was treated like family and not just a check book. I had a few cases going on 3 being criminal and 1 being an auto injury case, I shopped around for attorneys and when it came to the criminal matters (Super Drunk DUI, Loitering with intent, and operating vehicle with illegal equipment)  after speaking to the attorneys I was sure  that I would be going to jail for a minimum of 3-6months with a year or two probation and at the same time have my license revoked/suspended and ignation Interlock. I had a major brain injury last year and honestly was about to give up and felt like i lost the last bit of myself that is left, I couldn’t handle the answers I was getting from other attorneys I felt like my life was over and really  didn’t want to continue with it. Luckily I have Farris and his team like I stated before they treated me like family from day one and assured me they would keep me out of jail, I felt like I was reborn after speaking to Farris almost all the stress I had vanished in an instance. Farris and his team set up a strategized game plan to knock the cases out in the most aggressive but economical way possible, Farris laid out a few routes we could could take and directly got to executing said plan. Literally an All-Star team they got me the best possible outcome, I couldn’t believe it 2 of the 3 cases were completely dismissed, and the Super drunk DUI (High BAC way over .17 around a .223 which is a felony dui entails 6 months of jail, 2 years probation, 6+ points on record, crazy high tickets and a license revocation for a minimum of a year after which a blow device would need to be installed if I wanted to drive in the future is what i was expecting), I basically dug myself into a crazy deep whole talking to the officer at the time but Farris did his magic and somehow got it dropped to a misdemeanor OWVI and would have had it totally dropped if it weren’t for my own mistake but he still managed to get it dropped as low as possible. No jail, no license revocation or suspension, I basically received a slap on the wrist thanks to Farris. I can’t begin to express my appreciation for Farris and his law firm, I’m so thankful they came into my life they made it a breeze for me so I could actually fully focus on healing from being injured in major accident. I was working with another attorney for the auto injury case if you can even call it working, he wasn’t getting much done literally did 5 hours of work in my case in 12 months, I was left to fend for myself and damn near lost everything in the process whilst my previous attorney wasn’t even answering my calls or emails. So I asked Farris if he and his law firm could take over and they agreed to take the case over and got straight to work. Like I said this is an All-Star team and Farris hooked me up with his best and in my opinion the best Injury attorney you could possibly ask for Attorney Michael Oblizajek, Mike got straight on the case and for the first time since my injury I felt like something was getting done. Previous attorney didn’t even try to push for any of the benefits I was entitled to. I was missing doctors appointments due to transportation and was forced to sleep in my car because I had no money for rent because the previous attorney pushed for nothing bit himself, I almost lost myself/my will to live, the previous attorney and the auto insurance caused me so much stress and anguish that I wanted to give up. Thank god I found Farris and his team once they got to it everything changed. I was set up with a case manager, transportation, wage loss benefits, back pay for prior transportation, prescription/insurance card all within a month something my previous lawyer couldn’t do in a year. If you want a Law firm that actually cares and will fight for you go with The Law Office of Farris F. Haddad & Associates. I guarantee you can’t go wrong and will be treated like family not $ like others do.
Excellent attorney

The green card process can be long, emotional, and at times overwhelming, but Aaron made us feel supported every step of the way. He was always accessible, whether it was answering late-night emails, preparing us for interviews, or explaining complicated legal language in ways we could understand. His attention to detail was incredible—nothing was overlooked, and every piece of documentation was meticulously reviewed.

What stood out the most was how deeply he cared—not just about the legal outcome, but about us as people. We never felt like just another case. Aaron’s compassion, patience, and dedication were truly above and beyond. He invested so much time and energy into making sure everything was perfect, and thanks to him, my wife is now a proud green card holder.

This experience has been life-changing for our family, and we owe so much of that to Aaron. If you are looking for an immigration lawyer who is not only brilliant at what he does but also deeply human in how he does it, Aaron is the one. We would recommend him a thousand times over.

He didn't think that we would be successful in bringing such a claim. I felt completely blind sided. He left me worse off than when I started. My concern is one why he didn't call me with this update I had to call him. My major concern is why it took so long to believe in his opinion that I didn't have a case. Max it should have taken 3 months as advised by another attorney. I am left with medical bills, work loss, and pain and suffering. What makes matters even worse he advised me not to speak with there insurance company last year because I had a lawyer. I am left in the negative because of this situation. I would suggest if you have a case take it elsewhere because you can get false intent or false hope from this law firm and left worse off than you started. Last time we spoke was in August 2022 via email. I had to find out on a call that I made to them that she no longer works there but she was great! I am sharing my tragedy in hopes that I can prevent this from happening to anyone else. He should have never strung me along for this long and definitely should not have taking my case if he didn't think he could win it. We all know a lawyer won't take a case if they don't believe they can win it. If they do take it and realize they can't win it, it does not take over a year. reviews.text:
I had the privilege of having Liz represent me in an exceptionally complex case—one that would deter many attorneys. From the very beginning, she approached the overwhelming situation with a collaborative spirit, working alongside me to untangle the intricacies involved.

What truly sets Liz apart is her ability to genuinely hear her clients. Unlike many others, she didn’t just listen to my concerns; she engaged with them. There were moments when she delivered hard truths, but her honesty was invaluable.Choosing an attorney is often a leap of faith, as it involves placing trust in someone who is essentially selling their services. I can confidently say that Liz will never deceive you.

When she commits to getting something done, she follows through with unwavering dedication. With Liz in your corner, you can expect nothing less than passionate advocacy.

She invests the time needed, going above and beyond to fight for what is right and just. You can count on her to stand beside you in the courtroom, exuding confidence as your legal counsel.

Liz handled all of my hearings, my trial, and every aspect of my case with fierce tenacity. You simply won't find a better attorney or a more dedicated advocate. I wholeheartedly encourage you to hire Liz—she will fight for you every step of the way.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Alie. It has been an honor representing you.

reviews.text:
In my personal observation, attorney Elizabeth Hohauser represents the kind of legal practice that makes you question where the profession lost its compass. From the opposing side, it often appeared that truth and integrity were flexible concepts — adjusted as needed to fit the narrative.

I understand that attorneys are paid to advocate, but in my view, there’s a difference between zealous representation and calculated theatrics. What I witnessed leaned far more toward the latter. It left me wondering whether justice was still part of the equation or just a convenient slogan.

I’m sharing this not out of resentment, but from a sense of civic duty — transparency matters, and people deserve to know what kind of conduct they might encounter.

And for what it’s worth, about 7.3 million people read my Google reviews. Word travels fast.

**to respond back to the law firms response - we got exactly what should have happened from the trust, but the grey areas should NOT have been written in there. Again, I do not recommend Simasko law, and I will tell everyone thinking of building a trust with them to stay away.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We appreciate all feedback and understand that navigating trust and estate matters can be emotional and complex. However, we feel it’s important to clarify that you were not a client of our law firm. Our professional obligation was to our client—the trustee—and our role was to guide them in administering the trust according to its legal terms and in full compliance with Michigan law.

We understand you may be disappointed with the outcome of your distribution. That said, the decisions regarding the trust’s administration were not ours to make, but were directed by the terms of the trust itself, which we were required to follow.

We strive to be responsive and transparent in our communications with all parties involved in trust matters. If you believe there was a misunderstanding, we are open to addressing it in a more appropriate and constructive setting. Our firm remains committed to upholding the highest standards of professionalism, even in difficult situations.
Thank you so much for your support of Simasko Law!

Also, when a fence encroaches upon someone's land, it constitutes the intentional tort of trespass--and the small claims division of district courts does not enjoy subject matter jurisdiction over intentional torts (see MCL 600.8424 online at https://www.legislature.mi.gov/Laws/MCL?objectName=mcl-600-8424).

I found myself in an extremely difficult legal situation, facing an embezzlement charge involving up to $20,000. The odds were heavily stacked against me, and I truly believed there was no path forward. But with God and Attorney Derek Miller on my side, the outcome far exceeded my expectations.

What initially began as a serious felony case was negotiated down to a misdemeanor (under $200). At the time, I thought that was a favorable outcome. However, Attorney Miller didn’t stop there. He looked me in the eye and said, “Absolutely not. We are trying to get through this together with no conviction on your record. Let’s request to move this case to circuit court and see another prosecutor. I will fight to get you a better offer.”

That moment was when I knew I had an elite, experienced attorney in my corner someone who truly cared about my future and wasn’t afraid to go the extra mile.

When we appeared at the circuit court hearing, I arrived 10 minutes early, only to find that Derek was already inside, actively engaging with court staff, reading the room, and making his presence known. That level of preparation and professionalism gave me tremendous peace of mind.

When my case was finally called, Derek Miller stood beside me and advocated strongly. The final resolution, reached between the judge, prosecutor, and Attorney Miller, involved restitution and a very mild probation  no drug testing, no surprise visits from a probation officer. Most importantly, once restitution is paid, all charges will be dropped and I will have no conviction on my record.

Thanks to Derek Miller, I have a second chance at life  and the opportunity to continue building the career I’ve worked so hard for as a Practice Manager of Operations.

If you or a loved one find yourselves in a difficult legal situation, I highly recommend reaching out to Attorney Derek Miller. I’m more than willing to share my experience  because I know firsthand how much of a difference it makes to have the right attorney fighting for you.

reviews.text:
Georgoulis Disability Group is the only legal team I will recommend. Navigating the complexities of Social Security Disability claims can be long and daunting. I wholeheartedly recommend Alex Georgoulis and Georgoulis Disability Group for anyone facing a Social Security Disability claim. Especially if your a VA rated Veteran who has struggled to maintain steady employment due to health issues.

From the outset, Alex demonstrated a profound understanding of the complexities of the Social Security system. He was incredibly patient, understanding, and genuinely compassionate as I described my challenging health situation and its significant impact on my life. He immediately recognized the severity of my condition and how it has directly affected my inability to work for the past five years.

What truly impressed me was Alex's meticulous attention to detail and his ability to translate my complex Department of Veteran Affairs medical history and intensive treatment into clear, compelling legal arguments. He grasped the nuances of my various therapeutic approaches, which demanded substantial daily effort and the continuous management of deeply ingrained issues. He effectively communicated how my prescribed medications and the persistent, debilitating nature of my symptoms prevent me from maintaining employment.

Alex was especially empathetic and reassuring when I discussed my anxieties, including the severe periods when my condition leaves me unable to function, my financial struggles, and my concerns about navigating the healthcare system. His understanding that my commitment to ongoing therapy is essential for my well-being was incredibly validating.

Throughout the entire process, Alex and his team were responsive, professional, and excellent communicators. They kept me informed every step of the way, patiently answered all my questions, and thoroughly prepared me for hearings, which significantly reduced my stress.

Thanks to Alex's expertise and unwavering dedication, my Social Security Claim resulted in a positive outcome after a couple of deniles which is normal in most cases. He skillfully navigated a daunting process with both competence and genuine care, allowing me to focus on my health and ongoing recovery.

If you are facing a Social Security Disability claim due to severe and complex health challenges, Alex Georgoulis is the advocate you need. He truly fights for his clients and achieves results.

With Gratitude,
John Weir
reviews.ownerResponse: John, this has been a very heartfelt message and we cant thank you enough! We are just happy that you finally got the benefits you deserve!

My experience with Canu Torrice Law, and Andrew Canu specifically, has been beyond TERRIBLE.  Andrew manipulated me into paying a huge retainer urgently without ever mentioning that it would be nonrefundable until after the transaction was processed. He has been impossible to get ahold of for weeks at a time, consistently over-billed me, and even charged the same rate for work done by his legal assistant as if he had done it himself.  As an example, I have been billed $420 by Andrew for sending a 342 word email, a 112 word follow up a week later when he never replied, and an 85 word response from him.  Apparently Andrew thinks it’s reasonable to charge an hour and 24 minutes to read and send a worthless reply to an email that you only had to send because he was MIA.  Sadly, this has been Andrew’s entire MO.  Another example is that I was billed $120 the day I retained Andrew, at his $300 rate, for an email consisting of 3 sentences that was sent by his assistant containing my credit card receipt, a fee agreement and intake paperwork.   I have never been billed by an attorney for this type of clerical work, let alone when performed by a legal assistant at an attorney’s hourly rate.  In total I have been billed almost $1,400 by this firm without ever actually having done any work on my case or having an initial meeting.  I have worked with so many attorneys, and none have ever even approached how bad Andrew Canu has been.  And I see another reviewer came here to say the same thing.

Since Thanksgiving, Andrew has completely refused to communicate with me regarding a refund of my retainer. I’ve gone weeks without any response from him or anyone at his office, and the only way I could even get him to reply to an email was by sending a fax—yes, a fax—to his office. This level of unprofessionalism is unacceptable, and I deeply regret ever hiring him. Save yourself the headache and avoid this attorney at all costs.
I would strongly recommend NOT hiring Andrew Canu !!  Very unprofessional and lacking in showing concern for clients.

I cannot recommend Peter enough to anyone in need of an extremely intelligent, articulate, and aggressive defense attorney. He is a true advocate for his clients, and his expertise was instrumental in securing a favorable outcome for me. Thank you, Peter!

Moreover, their professionalism and attention to detail were evident in every aspect of their work. They meticulously prepared all necessary documents, leaving no room for errors or oversights. This level of diligence and dedication to their clients' needs sets Canu Law apart from the rest.

What truly impressed me was the personal touch and empathy displayed by the entire team. They understood the emotional aspect of my situation and provided not only legal guidance but also emotional support, which made a significant difference during a challenging period.

Lastly, their fees were fair and transparent. I felt that I received excellent value for the quality of service I received.

In summary, I highly recommend Canu Torrice Law for anyone in need of legal services. I do not typically leave reviews, but I wish I would’ve personally read some of these comments and not just their current rating before going forward with this firm. I retained Mr. Fischer for a custody case, I thought his abrasive personality was what I needed and that I was getting someone who was willing to be my knowledge and backbone. While seemingly knowledgeable, I felt he was pushy on settling and don’t feel as if he was a true “backbone” for me at all. We discussed things he stated he would add to the custody agreement and evidently did not. I had to reach out to get Information regarding hearings because it was never sent to me. During the child support hearing, we discussed specifics and during the hearing he went in a complete opposite direction than what was discussed causing me to miss out on hundreds of dollars of backpaid child support. Ironically, I was then sent a bill which included a fee for a prior to hearing phone call that I was charged 30 mins, and when I questioned it (showing a 2 min time stamp for that call vs his claimed 30) I was advised that included the 28 mins for pulling my file and preparing for the hearing. However, had he actually utilized that time he probably would’ve remembered our initial conversation and truly been prepared for my hearing.

So to sum this up…if you’ve made reading this far…I would suggest finding somewhere else. I will personally not be using their services again and would not recommend.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Curesha: I will try to respond to the parts I understand in your complaint.

You were referred to me by the UAW with a charge of malicious use of a telecommunications device. You were charged with harassing a colleague from work with your phone. I substituted your prior lawyer who you claimed was not properly representing you.

I believed in your case based upon the fake CPS report that your “friend” (as you called her) made about you, the testimony given at a PPO hearing by her, and the many police reports she made against you. I was prepared to go to trial to assert your innocence.

You were a nervous client, but I tried to calm you in our more than 10 telephone conversations. This was hard since you suffered from a deep sense of paranoia. You made many unfounded claims, like how the system was biased in favor of your friend/colleague, how you were targeted because of your race (though you and your colleague share the same race), and how you believed that this prosecution was part of a large conspiracy by unnamed government officials who did not know you, but wanted to see you in jail.

Though I tried to represent you despite your difficulties, you destroyed our ability to work together on October 14, 2025. On that day, you called me to complain about your case. After we talked, you and I came to an understanding, and you were relaxed. But the same day, Sonya from the UAW called and claimed that you told the UAW that my office had missed seven of your court dates. This was obviously a lie because (1) we had been to court only three times, and (2) my office always showed up. We also presented the evidence that you provided to the prosecutor, and once the prosecution told us that the case would not be dismissed, we chose to proceed to trial. Sonya also explained how you never told the UAW that your referral was for a criminal case. Sonya acknowledged that I had satisfactorily rebutted your lies. After I called to confront you, you admitted that you had said these things in jest and were just stressed. I resolved to withdraw from your case after you cursed me (like many times before) and since you falsely accused me of misconduct.

On October 28, 2025, I appeared in court to withdraw from your case. I presented you with the materials you provided me with as well. You acknowledged receipt of these things on the record. That same day, you and Tammie from the UAW called Kimberly, another lawyer in my office, to complain about me again. You falsely claimed that we had not showed up to court, had not presented your evidence to the prosecution, and sought your personal demise. After Kimberly told me you called, I joined you all on the call. For 25 minutes, I explained your conduct, how you had lied before, and how I had withdrawn from your case that same day. I informed Tammie that two weeks prior, Sonya from the UAW had determined that I satisfactorily rebutted your claims. Tammie confirmed that I earned my $750 fee from you and that I did nothing wrong. The call was ended.

As for the $200, that was owed to the City of Warren to obtain videos for your case that were not provided to my office. You agreed to pay for these costs when you signed a contract with my office. A client paying for discovery is routine. By the time the invoice had come, I had withdrawn from your case. You were/are always responsible for these costs, which is why you were invoiced by the City.

As for the privilege you speak of, since you made two complaints, you waived any confidentiality, not me. Given your false accusations of misconduct, I defended myself in accordance with Michigan Rule of Professional Conduct 1.6(c)(5).

In short, you have always spun tales of woe and misconduct by everyone except you, but, as Tammie and Sonya found, they are untrue. I represent my clients diligently, and I ask you do the right thing and remove this defamatory review.

Angelo Donofrio

reviews.ownerResponse:
Allen: You had multiple cases in my office. However, our office has represented you many times, including to terminate your probation for committing child abuse against your daughter, which you were pleased with.
I probated both your mother and father’s estates. There were only two court dates in these two cases. In your mother’s case, the first court date was to get you and your sister appointed as co-personal representatives. We appeared together and this was done. At that point, we needed to distribute your mother’s retirement account to you. What you did not know was that the account listed your father as beneficiary, meaning we needed to probate his estate. When I told you this, you said to do whatever was necessary. This was always your attitude and you were generally pleasant.
Once we filed your father’s probate case, a court date was scheduled for late July 2025. However, you randomly called me and all lawyers “assholes.” I was shocked because it came unprovoked. However, you apologized to me. I still appeared on your father’s case to adjourn it so you could potentially sue your sister for theft from your parents. I did this after you fired me. I filed motions to withdraw from your cases, which is required (since the lawyer you claimed would substitute me out never did).
You also expressed frustration because you owned a home with your sister but wanted your sister to leave so you could sell it and keep all the sales proceeds. I informed you (on no less than four occasions) that this was not legal, and that if you wanted her to leave, that you must file a lawsuit for partition, but you refused. I also told you that, if the home was sold in a partition case, the proceeds would likely be distributed 50-50 minus expenditures made by you both.
You retained me to review and draft a purchase agreement for the purchase of a building in Waterford. I reached out to the seller(s) and tried to find out who would draft the agreement. However, the seller(s) were evasive, but the seller(s) asked that I do it. I say “seller(s)” since it turns out that you were dealing with two people who frequently pretended to be the other (and when they answered my calls). A purchase agreement was never drafted since the seller(s) did not provide me with terms, documents pertinent to ownership of the property and business (that occupied the building), or any other necessary details. The seller(s) eventually stopped responding to my calls and emails, which we discussed on several occasions. You then decided to sue them though you gave them money with no contract, so I researched how you could sue. However, given what happened (and since you gave money with no written agreement) you could not sue the seller(s) since the statute of frauds requires a written and signed purchase agreement (with no exceptions in your case). You agreed with my analysis and decided to forego that lawsuit. You also told me in late June 2025 that you wanted another attorney sue the seller(s), and you never expressed displeasure at that point, so I do not know why you are complaining now. You also say that the things I did “contract-wise” seemed like Chat GPT created them, but I do not know what you mean since a contract was not drafted.
I also drafted an operating agreement for your company that I incorporated with the State of Michigan. You needed this document for an asset sale, but I advised you against it, and you agreed.
I spoke with you quite often during your ordeals, and you happily paid your bills, but then accused me of wasting time. You also accused me of not providing you with documents or copies of your signed contracts, but you admitted that was untrue when you fired me, and after I showed you an email I sent you with the signed agreements (which you told me to keep on one occasion). You always agreed with my legal analysis, even if you did not like the answers. Your accusations are malicious, or worse, delusional.
