AMAZING Service.

Just before Christmas I got into an incident where a driver continued to accelerate and break in front of me as we were in a turning lane, where we had the right of way to go, which caused a fender bender.

This had to be the WORST Christmas gift I could receive. I was so stressed because my life was on the right track, I had gotten a steady pace in my job with hours and an income increase, etc. I thought it was going to be a simple ticket, but it wasn't. I got a failure to clear an assured distance accident ticket, even though I was driving safe and secure, and there was no way I could avoid the person in front of me accelerating and going in the turning lane. The next day my cat died. Additionally, previously, I had just got a new car and because of it my car insurance had increased, so I didn't need any increase in expenses or stress.

I did my research and saw that the McCandliss law firm handles cases like this. I sent a message to inquire about their services, I received a phone call with a voicemail and a text (BONUS) that followed up with my specific inquiry (Which is rare, services tend to ask you to repeat your inquiry as if they didn't read it in the 1st place), which impressed me. I'm introverted, so sometimes handling business over the phone is a bit uncomfortable. I also received an email. I was asked about a follow-up phone call, and was also given the option to finish business via email or text, I opted for email. This was great for me because I work full-time long hours, and help out with my younger sister.

I spoke to Lisa via email, and she told me what to expect, said that she believes Mr. McCandliss could get me out of my ticket pretty easily, and quoted me a price, with a discount from using their website.
NOTE: The pricing is VERY reasonable, and of course, winning would save me thousands in car insurance.

I made the payment about a week later, and that was it. Mr. Candliss quickly scheduled me a court date, which was 1-2 months away, so I got to be stress-free over Christmas and New Years. I didn't even have to go to the court date. Approaching, the court date, I was a bit nervous because I hadn't spoken to Mr. Candliss over the phone, but I was assured all the information she had gathered from me was prior was adequate, and I would be called on the court day, more than likely. The day came, and within an hour, I was called, and a voicemail was left since I was unavailable taking my sister to work. I was given the potential solution, an amendment, I agreed to it, and that was it. The case was thrown out and I got a much lesser charge that I can deal with.

Lisa and Mr. Candliss really saved the day. I could not afford the cost of a higher insurance rate, and the potential to not drive because my job depends on it, and I am my younger sister's transportation to work. Lisa was so nice, and was so professional. Mr. Candliss told me after I agreed to the amendment that I would receive an email about it, with the payment instructions for the lesser amendment charge. I received that email QUICKLY, like within an hour, with pictures of the paperwork for the case of the amendment charge, a scan from the court of the amendment, etc. The BEST business I've ever done. Money, well spent! As a black man in America, I'm often scared that justice will not work in my favor, but for once, I am confident that good things to help to good people, and with the right lawyer, you can fight on the side of justice and win. If you come across their services, use them. I have recommended all my friends who may need McCandliss' firm's law services.

Glenn McCandliss was a key part of getting my license back after having it revoked for over one year. His office is very responsive to any questions or concerns that I had along the way, and always responded promptly. I have had - and heard about from others - some very poor experiences with lawyers, who - although they may have done some good work - really leave their clients feeling like they are hanging out in the wind; they charge far too much, and then fail to communicate, and spend almost zero time in helping their clients prepare to approach their hearings and court dates with confidence. Glenn was the opposite of all that, and I would recommend him to anyone with drivers license issues.

I have been using Mr. McCandliss and his services for a few months now to handle an assortment of legal issues. From tickets to seized property His prices are also reasonable and has earned my trust. Glenn has shown himself to be diligent and professional.  As a smaller logistics company we do not have the resources to employ a full time attorney but Glenn treats us like he is exactly that. He is very fast to tell us when he feels his services are not necessary and gives us solid advice. He has yet to steer us in the wrong direction and has even helped out office personnel with private legal consultation. I highly recommend him to any company in Logistics and Trucking. He is just a good guy with an honest disposition and if you can handle blunt truth without the tip toeing around the issue, he is your man. I plan on using him exclusively in Michigan. The entire team made what could have been a stressful process feel straightforward and manageable. They explained every step clearly, answered all of my questions promptly, and were always available when I needed guidance or reassurance.

What stood out most was the personalized attention I received. The team took the time to truly understand my situation and goals, offering advice that was tailored specifically to me. It was clear they genuinely cared about helping me get a fresh start — not just completing paperwork.

I wholeheartedly recommend Kostopoulos & Associates to anyone considering bankruptcy. They are knowledgeable, compassionate, and incredibly professional. You’ll feel supported every step of the way.

Update: It’s been just over two years since my filing, and my credit is stronger than it’s been in decades. I was able to finance a new car only seven months after bankruptcy and a brand-new vehicle within two years. None of this would have been possible without Rita and her amazing team!

I retained Goldstar law to assist me with an employment discrimination case.
There is a 90 day window after an EEOC investigation to file a case in Federal court.
60 days into this deadline while reviewing the EEOC file they dropped my case and I was given this bit of news by some underling at the firm. The attorney would not speak directly with me. I asked if at the least they would help me prepare the documents to file on my own as I had zero legal experience but the abandonment was complete leaving me with less than 30 days to learn how to do it myself. Other attorney I contacted stated I had a good case but with only 30 days to file passed on representation.
Using the EEOC file information reviewed by a non attorney at the firm I figured it out and as a self represented person I settled my claim against a governmental agency for $125,000. Myself, a non attorney…

I retained this firm to represent me in a serious federal employment matter. Unfortunately, I experienced a disturbing lack of communication, disregard for deadlines, and violations of basic professional conduct. I wish I could give negative stars.

My attorneys failed to notify me of material conflicts, involved unauthorized individuals despite prior agreements, and eventually withdrew after causing significant harm to both my case and my health, including missed opportunities, stalled progress, aggravated emotional distress, and worsening heart-related symptoms.

They showed more concern for protecting each other than for protecting their client. They have no integrity.

After their withdrawal, I was forced to proceed pro se and clean up the damage myself. I’ve since filed formal grievances and am pursuing legal remedies.

I caution others to carefully review this firm’s practices before engaging them. The issues I faced are not isolated. They’re reflected in this firm’s pattern of low reviews and poor client treatment.

Their public persona does not match the experience of many clients, especially if you are not of European descent, according to multiple reviews here. hmmmm....

Benice was a delightful person to converse with.

I will give the Farm it’s full credit and what they were able to do with my case, for that alone on this part only I am eternally grateful. However, I Disclosed a pertinent fact about my case prior to the firm representing me For which I have proof of and credible witness to, the firm and Jennifer we’re fine with this person in fact for 10 months of my case only to look for a way out and leave my case based on that same fact. Jennifer, herself blasted me on the phone for trying to reach out to her boss Mr. Michael Pitt to try and get a better understanding and have a meeting with him and Jennifer to understand the legal complexities of my case. She felt that I was going behind her back but all I was trying to do was have a meeting with her boss and her To get a better understanding of things. The managing partner of the firm, I believe blatantly disregarded me as a client of the firm, failed to return my call or email to address my concerns. Doing so in my opinion shows a lack of total professionalism and a painful disgrace to the legal profession. I recently tried to amend my relationship with the firm, I’m doing so I also Disclosed a very personal story of my life and where I come from which I admit in part may have been a contributing factor for some of the discord between Jennifer and I. Instead of Jennifer exercising some genuine compassion and understanding For my personal story She just replied via email attorney-client relationship cannot be restored stop contacting me for me a man with a disability and wheelchair-bound who is already gone through so much that was hurtful, dismissive, rude and incredibly unprofessional. When we had discord before we worked on our discord prior to us having mediation where Jennifer called me and wanted to work things out prior to mediation. At that time we worked our differences professionally and everything was fine. Then they drop the case I wanted to wait for something I can prove they knew about for 10 months but they left me high and dry and when I reach out to try to make amends and see if we can work past this issue, I get told to pound sand to stop contacting her to which I respected her request. It is in my opinion full heartedly, That any individual with a physical disability or mental impairment not seek the services of this firm because of their apparent rudeness, disrespect and disregard for a person with a disability and their hurtful dismissive conduct

Randall was an outstanding attorney during my divorce. He was professional, knowledgeable, and always quick to respond whenever I had questions. He explained everything clearly and made a stressful process much easier to navigate. Thanks to Randall’s hard work and strategy, the final settlement was definitely in my favor. I’m extremely grateful for his guidance and would highly recommend him to anyone going through a divorce. Also, all his office staff was very easy to work with. The process of divorce is never an easy decision to make, but I’m extremely grateful I had the best on my team.
Randy and his team are spectacular - they're clear with how they can help and follow through with what they offer. Straight-up legends - thank you!

Having finished a contentious divorce, I cannot recommend Randy and his team more highly. Randy made himself available 24/7 to assist with concerns about my case, and offered well-explained, concise, and extremely helpful (which I did not see until my divorce was finalized) advice that maximized every part of the divorce process in my favor. He was available for any question I had, big or small, and did not once divert questions to a secretary or paralegal.

Randy’s team is professional, timely, and well-organized; any time an issue came up in my case, I was immediately advised of the issue, and Randy took care to reach out to me personally to ask how I would like to proceed.

Randy’s work ethic and commitment to his clients are unparalleled. Even though my divorce finalization was a minor procedural matter, Randy still called me a few hours later to ensure there were no lingering questions and that I was satisfied with the outcome.

Randy’s billing rates are more than reasonable - as I told him (after our business was concluded), I would happily have paid more for his services had I known in advance how excellent he is in his craft and how adept he would be in ensuring a quick and favorable outcome for me.

Thank you, Randy - you made a part of my life that is typically confusing and terrible into something that was understandable, simple, and beneficial to me. You treated me as more than a client - you treated me as a friend and as an equal, and it was apparent at every step in the process you were making a genuine (and effective!) effort to secure my best interest.

10/10 for Chioini and everyone on his team, I am more than happy to put my name on a recommendation for him and his services and I would be shocked if anyone had feedback worse than that.

Thank you Randy, I appreciate you.
Highly Recommend! Amazing Team!
Great firm highly recommend

Thank you from bottom of my heart Randy, and all the staff members at Chioni Group!!

I had an excellent experience working with Randy. He is extremely knowledgeable and thorough, and takes the time to explain strategy in a clear and thoughtful way. What stood out most was how communicative and attentive he is—you never feel left hanging, and you can trust that he’ll get back to you quickly with solid guidance. His ability to anticipate challenges and think strategically makes him an essential part of any divorce team. I highly recommend him for anyone navigating this process.

They listen & are responsive, knowledgeable & able to explain complex legal matters in a way that those of us who are not lawyers can understand. I have been very happy to recommend this firm to my family, friends and neighbors.

I can't recommend Randall Chioini and his team highly enough. I am extremely impressed with his knowledge and integrity. He's gone above and beyond to help my family with a very complicated and serious custody situation. If you need an honest attorney, Mr. Chioini is your man.
So knowledgable and quick to reply! Very helpful and would highly recommend!
I was a Realtor in a transaction helping a client sell and needed last minute assistance for him. They did a fabulous job in helping us keep the closing on track!

The whole process of dealing with a divorce is never an easy experience. Attorney Randy Chioni was fantastic during my entire divorce process from the get go. He gave me space to make my own decisions but at times when I wasn’t at my strongest, he definitely took over to address time sensitive issues. (Looking back now, I am very thankful for that because my decision would have been based on emotions rather than knowledge.) He always advised on the consequences involved when making any decision.

During our initial meeting, he spent time understanding me as a person. He put together puzzle pieces about my marriage, my children, my lifestyle, my job, and my necessities to better predict a reasonable outcome that would fit my needs. I was never promised any outcome but with all honesty everything that mattered to me, was addressed better than I expected.

He is exceptionally knowledgeable on how to negotiate with the opposing attorney and the referee during mediation. His team does a great job to relay messages in a timely manner.

Randy and his team don’t waste time on little details that do not matter in court. I have never found billing errors on the time that I was billed. As a matter of fact, there were certain situations that he spent time on, that I was not billed for. (It would say credited from Attorney on my billing statement)

After my divorce was finalized, I had to re-finance the marital home without ever being on the title or mortgage. Little did I know, Randy put one sentence in my divorce decree that enabled me to assume the mortgage. Basically, this meant I can take over the exact mortgage which had a low 3% interest rate verses today’s market of a 7% interest rate. I never discussed this with him because I knew it wasn’t possible with a conventional loan.  This was a sentence in fine print that later looking back at the docket, it saved me thousands of dollars. He protects you in the final judgement in every way possible.

Overall, I always felt that Randy was confident in every move he took. He has great passion and experience in this field.

I recently reached out to the Chioini Group for assistance with a legal matter. Although they were unable to take on my case, Mr. Randall Chioini generously spent half an hour on the phone with me, offering invaluable advice and guidance—all without charging a fee. His professionalism, compassion, and genuine concern for my situation were evident throughout our conversation. It's clear that the Chioini Group is dedicated to providing top-tier, premium representation, especially in complex divorce litigation and custody matters. Their commitment to clients is further demonstrated by their AV-Preeminent Rating from Martindale-Hubbell and recognition as "Super Lawyers" by Thomson Reuters. If I ever require legal assistance in the future, particularly for a divorce matter, Mr. Chioini will be my first call.
Chioni and his team are very helpful, professional, patient and prompt. Definitely recommend!
Extremely happy with how you and your firm helped us through this trying time. Thank you!

Randy and his team are my heroes! Going through divorce is tough enough, but when you have the best representation, it makes the process less painful/stressful. Randy not only had great advice and direction, but he also listened to my questions and concerns with zero judgment. I highly recommend!

Randy is an outstanding attorney. He guided me through a challenging and complex divorce with expertise and clarity. He was highly responsive to all inquiries and provided clear direction on what I needed to do to achieve the best outcome.

The best decision I made in my divorce was hiring Randy.  Some law firms wouldn't even do a consultation with me once I told them that we owned a couple (small) businesses.
